data_description.device_profile: Select the matching device profile for your Zendure system. The profile contains tuned control parameters.
data_description.soc_entity: Sensor with the current state of charge (SoC) of the battery in percent. This is the central basis for all decisions.
data_description.soc_limit_entity: Optional sensor from the Zendure integration that reports active BMS limits, such as charge or discharge lockouts.
data_description.pack_capacity_kwh: Usable capacity of a single battery pack in kWh. It is multiplied by the number of installed packs to calculate the total capacity.
data_description.battery_ac_power_entity: Sensor for the battery AC power. The value must be positive during discharge. Recommended: sensor.solarflow_2400_ac_bat_in_out
data_description.additional_battery_charge_entity: Optional sensor of a second battery system. If this sensor reports positive charge power, Battery SmartFlow AI blocks discharge.
data_description.capacity_entity: Optional sensor with the automatically calculated available battery capacity in kWh, for example from the Zendure integration. If set, it overrides the manual calculation.
data_description.installed_pv_wp: Installed theoretical PV module power of the system in Wp. This value is used for relative summer/winter thresholds and other PV-related logic.
data_description.pv_entity: Sensor with the current PV power in watts. Used to detect surplus.
data_description.pv_forecast_today_entity: Optional sensor with today’s PV daily forecast, for example from Solcast PV Forecast. The forecast is only an additional planning input and not required for the integration.
data_description.pv_forecast_tomorrow_entity: Optional sensor with tomorrow’s PV daily forecast, for example from Solcast PV Forecast. The forecast is only an additional planning input and not required for the integration.
data_description.price_now_entity: Sensor with the current electricity price, for example Tibber. Used for economic decisions.
data_description.price_export_entity: Sensor with price forecast data, for example Tibber / EPEX. Enables price-based pre-planning.
data_description.ckw_enabled: Enables direct fetching of hourly CKW dynamic tariff prices via API. No separate HA sensor needed. Prices are automatically refreshed every 30 minutes.
data_description.currency: Select CHF for Swiss Francs (e.g. for CKW tariff) or EUR for Euro-based tariffs.
data_description.grid_mode: Choose how your grid consumption is measured: one combined sensor or separate sensors for import and export.
data_description.grid_power_entity: A single sensor with positive power for grid import and negative power for grid export.
data_description.grid_import_entity: Sensor with current grid import power, positive value only.
data_description.grid_export_entity: Sensor with current grid export power, positive value only.
data_description.ac_mode_entity: Select entity from the Zendure integration that switches between charging (INPUT) and discharging (OUTPUT).
data_description.input_limit_entity: Number entity for setting the charge power in watts.
data_description.output_limit_entity: Number entity for setting the discharge power in watts.

reconfigure_grid.title: Configure grid metering
reconfigure_grid.description: Select the grid sensors matching the mode

data_description.grid_power_entity: A single sensor with positive power for import and negative power for export.
data_description.grid_import_entity: Sensor with current grid import power in watts.
data_description.grid_export_entity: Sensor with current grid export power in watts.

grid.title: Configure grid metering
grid.description: Select the grid sensors matching the mode

data_description.grid_power_entity: A single sensor with positive power for import and negative power for export.
data_description.grid_import_entity: Sensor with current grid import power in watts.
data_description.grid_export_entity: Sensor with current grid export power in watts.

data_description.device_profile: Select the matching device profile for your Zendure system. The control parameters are adjusted automatically.
data_description.soc_entity: Sensor with the current battery state of charge in percent.
data_description.soc_limit_entity: Optional status sensor from the Zendure integration for detecting active BMS lockouts.
data_description.pack_capacity_kwh: Usable capacity of a single battery pack in kWh. It is multiplied by the number of installed packs to calculate the total capacity.
data_description.battery_ac_power_entity: Sensor for the battery AC power. The value must be positive during discharge. Recommended: sensor.solarflow_2400_ac_bat_in_out
data_description.additional_battery_charge_entity: Optional sensor of a second battery system. If this sensor reports positive charge power, Battery SmartFlow AI blocks discharge.
data_description.capacity_entity: Optional sensor with the automatically calculated available battery capacity in kWh, for example from the Zendure integration. If set, it overrides the manual calculation.
data_description.installed_pv_wp: Installed theoretical PV module power of the system in Wp. This value is used for relative summer/winter thresholds and other PV-related logic.
data_description.pv_entity: Sensor with the current PV power in watts.
data_description.pv_forecast_today_entity: Optional sensor with today’s PV daily forecast, for example from Solcast PV Forecast. The forecast is only an additional planning input and not required for the integration.
data_description.pv_forecast_tomorrow_entity: Optional sensor with tomorrow’s PV daily forecast, for example from Solcast PV Forecast. The forecast is only an additional planning input and not required for the integration.
data_description.price_now_entity: Current electricity price. Enables price-based discharge.
data_description.price_export_entity: Price forecast data for the next hours. Enables intelligent charge planning.
data_description.ckw_enabled: Enables direct fetching of hourly CKW dynamic tariff prices via API. No separate HA sensor needed. Prices are automatically refreshed every 30 minutes.
data_description.currency: Select CHF for Swiss Francs (e.g. for CKW tariff) or EUR for Euro-based tariffs.
data_description.grid_mode: Defines how grid import and export are measured.
data_description.grid_power_entity: A combined grid sensor (positive = import, negative = export).
data_description.ac_mode_entity: Zendure AC mode (INPUT/OUTPUT). It is controlled automatically by the integration.

init.title: Edit control profile
init.description: Select the area you want to edit. “General” contains shared profile parameters, “Charge” contains the control values for charge power, “Discharge” contains the control values for discharge power, and “Expert mode” contains additional protection and diagnostic functions.

general.title: Edit control profile – General
general.description: General control parameters of the active device profile.

data_description.installed_pv_wp: Installed theoretical PV module power of the system in Wp. This value is used for relative summer/winter thresholds and other PV-related logic.
data_description.TARGET_IMPORT_W: Target value for a small intended grid import. A higher value reduces the risk of unwanted export but slightly increases grid consumption.
data_description.EXPORT_GUARD_W: Additional safety margin against unwanted export. Higher values make the control more cautious.
data_description.KEEPALIVE_MIN_DEFICIT_W: Minimum deficit from which discharge is kept active. Helps prevent flapping at very low loads.
data_description.KEEPALIVE_MIN_OUTPUT_W: Minimum discharge power for keeping discharge stable. Prevents unnecessary switching on and off.
data_description.SOC_DISCHARGE_RESUME_MARGIN: Additional SoC distance above the configured minimum SoC that must be reached before discharge is released again after an SoC lockout. Prevents flapping between charge and discharge at low battery levels, especially in the morning with fluctuating PV power.

charge.title: Edit control profile – Charge
charge.description: Control parameters for the charging behavior of the active device profile.

data_description.CHARGE_DEADBAND_W: Tolerance range for charge control in which no adjustment is made. A higher value makes charging calmer, a lower value reacts faster and more precisely.
data_description.CHARGE_KP_UP: Gain when increasing charge power. Higher values increase charge power faster but may lead to less stable behavior.
data_description.CHARGE_KP_DOWN: Gain when reducing charge power. Higher values reduce charge power faster but may cause stronger jumps.
data_description.CHARGE_MAX_STEP_UP: Maximum power step per control cycle when increasing charge power. Higher values make charge control faster but potentially more aggressive.
data_description.CHARGE_MAX_STEP_DOWN: Maximum power step per control cycle when reducing charge power. Higher values slow down charging faster but may feel less smooth.

discharge.title: Edit control profile – Discharge
discharge.description: Control parameters for the discharging behavior of the active device profile.

data_description.DISCHARGE_DEADBAND_W: Tolerance range for discharge control in which no adjustment is made. A higher value makes discharge calmer, a lower value reacts faster and more precisely.
data_description.DISCHARGE_KP_UP: Gain when increasing discharge power. Higher values increase discharge power faster but may overshoot more easily.
data_description.DISCHARGE_KP_DOWN: Gain when reducing discharge power. Higher values reduce discharge power faster but may behave less smoothly.
data_description.DISCHARGE_MAX_STEP_UP: Maximum power step per control cycle when increasing discharge power. Higher values make discharge control faster but potentially more aggressive.
data_description.DISCHARGE_MAX_STEP_DOWN: Maximum power step per control cycle when reducing discharge power. Higher values slow down discharge faster but may cause stronger jumps.

expert.title: Edit control profile – Expert mode
expert.description: Advanced protection and diagnostic functions. First enable expert mode. Afterwards, cell-voltage protection can optionally be enabled. When cell-voltage protection is active, additional sensors matching the configured number of battery packs are displayed.

data_description.expert_mode_enabled: Enables the advanced expert area for additional protection and diagnostic functions.

expert_cell_voltage.title: Edit control profile – Cell-voltage protection
expert_cell_voltage.description: Optionally enable cell-voltage protection. If enabled, the next step configures the cell-voltage sensors and protection thresholds.

data_description.cell_voltage_protection_enabled: Enables cell-voltage-based battery protection. Afterwards, the sensors for the lowest cell voltage per battery pack and the protection thresholds can be configured.

expert_cell_voltage_config.title: Edit control profile – Cell-voltage configuration
expert_cell_voltage_config.description: Configure the sensors for the lowest cell voltage per battery pack and the associated protection thresholds.

data_description.lowest_cell_voltage_pack_1: Sensor for the lowest cell voltage of the first battery pack. This value is used for the global protection assessment.
data_description.lowest_cell_voltage_pack_2: Sensor for the lowest cell voltage of the second battery pack. This value is used for the global protection assessment.
data_description.lowest_cell_voltage_pack_3: Sensor for the lowest cell voltage of the third battery pack. This value is used for the global protection assessment.
data_description.lowest_cell_voltage_pack_4: Sensor for the lowest cell voltage of the fourth battery pack. This value is used for the global protection assessment.
data_description.lowest_cell_voltage_pack_5: Sensor for the lowest cell voltage of the fifth battery pack. This value is used for the global protection assessment.
data_description.lowest_cell_voltage_pack_6: Sensor for the lowest cell voltage of the sixth battery pack. This value is used for the global protection assessment.
data_description.cell_voltage_warning: Threshold for critically low cell voltage. Used to trigger early emergency charging or protective measures.
data_description.cell_voltage_cutoff: Hard lower limit for cell voltage. If reached or undershot, discharge is locked.
data_description.cell_voltage_resume: Resume threshold above the discharge lockout. Discharge is released again only after this voltage has been reached.

state.planning_latest_start: Price charging at latest start time
state.planning_forecast_poor: Price charging due to poor PV forecast
state.planning_forecast_mixed: Price charging with mixed PV forecast
state.planning_reality_override: Price charging due to weak live PV despite good forecast
state.valley_boost_mixed: Price valley charging, reduced due to forecast
state.valley_opportunity_mixed: Opportunistic valley charging with mixed forecast

state.expensive_discharge: Discharging due to high electricity price
state.very_expensive_force_discharge: Forced discharge due to extreme price peak
state.planning_latest_start: Price charging started (latest reasonable start time reached)
state.planning_forecast_poor: Price charging due to poor PV forecast
state.planning_forecast_mixed: Price charging with mixed PV forecast
state.valley_opportunity_charge: Price valley used because real PV is barely available
state.valley_opportunity_charge_mixed_forecast: Price valley used with mixed forecast and weak real PV
state.valley_boost_charge_mixed_forecast: Price valley detected, but reduced due to mixed PV forecast
state.planning_forecast_reality_override: Price charging started: real PV too weak despite good forecast
state.very_cheap_force_charge: Very cheap threshold reached: maximum charging active
state.additional_battery_charging_block: Additional battery is charging: discharge blocked
state.soc_min_resume_block: Discharge blocked (resume threshold not reached yet)
state.pv_charge_blocked_by_discharge_protectio: PV charging blocked because discharge is still locked by SoC/cell protection
